title: Podman Doesn't Honor Dockerfile Syntax
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman does not honor valid `Dockerfile` syntax. Rootless builds.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

The following Dockerfile is valid:

Dockerfile:

```
$ cat Dockerfile
ARG TAG
FROM internal.mirror/alpine:${TAG}
RUN echo foo
```

Does not work with `podman`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman build --build-arg TAG=3.6 .
error determining starting point for build: no FROM statement found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
docker build --build-arg TAG=3.6 .
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/3 : ARG TAG
Step 2/3 : FROM internal.mirror/alpine:${TAG}
3.6: Pulling from alpine
3489d1c4660e: Pull complete
Digest: sha256:5abd4fccfca1f23481990d1f9a49d70901cd01fac5ae3f45af3c1f7d877e9644
Status: Downloaded newer image for internal.mirror/alpine:3.6
 ---> 94627dfbdf19
Step 3/3 : RUN echo foo
 ---> Running in ec903603c6c7
foo
Removing intermediate container ec903603c6c7
 ---> 4524f803d29c
Successfully built 4524f803d29c
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:       0.11.1.1
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.2
  podman version: 0.11.1.1
host:

    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: d61f1d176b2b01a5e46efbf236427ece745b92f2-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 1744646144
  MemTotal: 52590579712
  OCIRuntime:
    package: containerd.io-1.2.6-3.3.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8
      commit: 425e105d5a03fabd737a126ad93d62a9eeede87f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 4294156288
  SwapTotal: 4294963200
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: 5df708eee7e5
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.1.3.el7.x86_64
  os: linux

  uptime: 621h 2m 4.69s (Approximately 25.88 days)

    number: 0
  RunRoot: /tmp/user/1000
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-0.11.1.1-3.git594495d.el7.centos.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Smells like an i/o issue.  Can you try adding quotes around the value ala:

```
podman build --build-arg TAG="3.6" .
```

No dice unfortunately:

```
$ podman build --build-arg TAG="3.6" .
error determining starting point for build: no FROM statement found
```
@ajatman well rats, was hoping that would be a quick work around.  Thanks for the test.
This is an ancient version of podman.  Is it from RHEL7?  I believe this is fixed in the upstream version

> This is an ancient version of podman. Is it from RHEL7? I believe this is fixed in the upstream version

Oh excellent, yes it is Centos 7 with RPMs backed via a corporate Spacwalk instance so it's quite possible I have to got deal with some red tape. 

I'll test and update, thanks!

That was the ticket:

```
[worker@5dc7e7d97592 test]$ podman version
Version:            1.4.4

Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
[worker@5dc7e7d97592 test]$ podman info --debug

  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 622h 42m 45.53s (Approximately 25.92 days)
registries:
  blocked: null

  search:
  - registry.centos.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com

Results with:

```
$ cat Dockerfile
ARG TAG
FROM internal.mirror/alpine:${TAG}
RUN echo foo
[worker@5dc7e7d97592 test]$ podman build --build-arg TAG=3.6 .
ERRO[0000] unable to write system event: "write unixgram @0008e->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: no such file or directory"

Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 3489d1c4660e done
Copying config 94627dfbdf done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN echo foo
foo
STEP 3: COMMIT
cc103c6852f35a82fbc35f998d2e64161f1f0e02e9279d97cedc3acf8991fa4a

Thanks for the help & easy fix!
